2605.04808.title: DecodingTrust-Agent Platform (DTap): A Controllable and Interactive Red-Teaming Platform for AI Agents
2605.04808.paper_summary: AI agents are increasingly deployed across diverse domains to automate complex workflows through long-horizon and high-stakes action executions. Due to their high capability and flexibility, such agents raise significant security and safety concerns. A growing number of real-world incidents have shown that adversaries can easily manipulate agents into performing harmful actions, such as leaking API keys, deleting user data, or initiating unauthorized transactions. Evaluating agent security is inherently challenging, as agents operate in dynamic, untrusted environments involving external tools, heterogeneous data sources, and frequent user interactions. However, realistic, controllable, and reproducible environments for large-scale risk assessment remain largely underexplored. To address this gap, we introduce the DecodingTrust-Agent Platform (DTap), the first controllable and interactive red-teaming platform for AI agents, spanning 14 real-world domains and over 50 simulation environments that replicate widely used systems such as Google Workspace, Paypal, and Slack. To scale the risk assessment of agents in DTap, we further propose DTap-Red, the first autonomous red-teaming agent that systematically explores diverse injection vectors (e.g., prompt, tool, skill, environment, combinations) and autonomously discovers effective attack strategies tailored to varying malicious goals. Using DTap-Red, we curate DTap-Bench, a large-scale red-teaming dataset comprising high-quality instances across domains, each paired with a verifiable judge to automatically validate attack outcomes. Through DTap, we conduct large-scale evaluations of popular AI agents built on various backbone models, spanning security policies, risk categories, and attack strategies, revealing systematic vulnerability patterns and providing valuable insights for developing secure next-generation agents.
2605.04808.paper_summary_zh: AI 代理人越來越多地被部署在各種領域，以通過長期和高風險的行動執行來自動化複雜的工作流程。由於其高能力和靈活性，這些代理人引發了重大的安全和安全性擔憂。越來越多的現實事件顯示，對手可以輕易操縱代理人執行有害行為，例如洩露 API 密鑰、刪除用戶數據或發起未經授權的交易。評估代理人的安全性本質上是具有挑戰性的，因為代理人在動態、不受信任的環境中運作，涉及外部工具、異構數據源和頻繁的用戶互動。然而，用於大規模風險評估的現實、可控和可重現的環境仍然在很大程度上未被探索。為了解決這一空白，我們介紹了 DecodingTrust-Agent Platform (DTap)，這是首個可控和互動的 AI 代理人紅隊平台，涵蓋 14 個現實世界領域和超過 50 個模擬環境，這些環境複製了廣泛使用的系統，如 Google Workspace、Paypal 和 Slack。為了擴大 DTap 中代理人的風險評估，我們進一步提出了 DTap-Red，這是首個自主紅隊代理人，系統性地探索多樣的注入向量（例如，提示、工具、技能、環境、組合），並自主發現針對不同惡意目標的有效攻擊策略。使用 DTap-Red，我們策劃了 DTap-Bench，這是一個大規模的紅隊數據集，包含來自各個領域的高質量實例，每個實例都配有可驗證的評審，以自動驗證攻擊結果。通過 DTap，我們對基於各種骨幹模型構建的流行 AI 代理人進行大規模評估，涵蓋安全政策、風險類別和攻擊策略，揭示系統性脆弱性模式，並為開發安全的下一代代理人提供有價值的見解。
2605.04808.author: Zhaorun Chen et.al.
2605.04808.authors: Zhaorun Chen, Xun Liu, Haibo Tong, Chengquan Guo, Yuzhou Nie, Jiawei Zhang, Mintong Kang, Chejian Xu, Qichang Liu, Xiaogeng Liu, Tianneng Shi, Chaowei Xiao, Sanmi Koyejo, Percy Liang, Wenbo Guo, Dawn Song, Bo Li
